patterns.name: Debug Mode Enabled
patterns.description: Debug mode is enabled — exposes stack traces, internal paths, and sensitive data to attackers

patterns.name: CORS Wildcard
patterns.description: CORS allows all origins (*) — any website can make authenticated requests to your API

patterns.name: Bind All Interfaces
patterns.description: Binding to 0.0.0.0 — service is accessible from any network interface

patterns.name: Default Database Password
patterns.description: Default database password detected — databases with weak passwords are easily compromised

patterns.name: Weak SECRET_KEY
patterns.description: SECRET_KEY is too short (under 20 chars) — weak keys can be brute-forced

patterns.name: Common Default SECRET_KEY
patterns.description: SECRET_KEY is set to a known default — attackers can forge sessions and tokens

patterns.name: SSL Verification Disabled
patterns.description: SSL verification disabled — connections are vulnerable to man-in-the-middle attacks

patterns.name: Insecure Cookie Settings
patterns.description: Insecure cookie settings — cookies may be intercepted or accessed via JavaScript
